title: NA
text: (functions optimized agentic use, can called directly without arguments.)
code: # Executing code devtools::load_all() code  # Tests devtools::test() # all tests devtools::test(filter = "^{name}") # tests for files starting with {name} devtools::test_active_file("R/{name}.R") # tests for R/{name}.R devtools::test_active_file("R/{name}.R", desc = 'blah') # single test with exact description "blah" (no regexp)  # Test coverage devtools::test_coverage() # all files devtools::test_coverage_active_file("R/{name}.R") # coverage for R/{name}.R from tests in tests/testthat/test-{name}.R  # Documentation devtools::document() # redocument package pkgdown::check_pkgdown() # check website  # Run complete R CMD check devtools::check()

title: NA
text: three possible ways run code, listed rough order desirability: ’re running inside Posit Assistant otherwise executeCode() tool available, use run code session user can also interact . Otherwise, R REPL (e.g. mcp__r__repl btw::run_r) available, use . Note mcp__r__repl uses sandbox blocks network requests reads/writes outside current directory. Otherwise, use Rscript -e "code".

title: NA
text: Follow tidyverse style guide Always run air format . generating code. Use base pipe operator (|>), magrittr pipe (%>%). Use \() ... single-line anonymous functions. cases, use function() {...}.

title: NA
text: Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test-{name}.R. new code accompanying test. existing tests, place new tests next similar existing tests. Strive keep tests minimal comments. Never put code test-{name}.R file outside test_that() block. Instead, use tests/testthat/helper.R tests/testthat/helper-{name}.R. Avoid expect_true() expect_false() favor specific expectation better failure message. expectations newer releases might know expect_all_true(), expect_all_equal(), expect_r6_class(). use expect_error() expect_warning() error warning known class. Generally, prefer expect_snapshot(error = TRUE) errors expect_snapshot() warnings allow user review full text output. Avoid .package argument local_mocked_bindings(); modifies namespace another package, good practice. Instead create mockable version function current package. See ?local_mocked_bindings details.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing function exported roxygen2 documentation. Internal functions roxygen documentation. Wrap roxygen2 comments 80 characters. Whenever add new (non-internal) documentation topic, also add topic _pkgdown.yml. Always re-document package changing roxygen2 comment. Use pkgdown::check_pkgdown() check topics included reference index.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing change given bullet NEWS.md. Small documentation changes. Internal refactorings. Fixes bugs introduced current dev version. bullet briefly describe change end user mention related issue parentheses. bullet can consist multiple sentences contain newlines (.e. line wrap). change related function, put name function early bullet. change related issue, include issue number parentheses. include GitHub username PR created someone isn’t author. Order bullets alphabetically function name. Put bullets don’t mention function names beginning.

title: NA
text: need deprecate function argument? Read output usethis::learn_tidy_skill("deprecate"). adding input checking existing function writing new exported function? Read output usethis::learn_tidy_skill("arg-checking").

title: NA
text: user asks commit, use markdown commit message, don’t line wrap. commit fixes issue, include Fixes #num. line. push user explicitly requests .

title: NA
text: Use sentence case headings. Use US English.

title: NA
text: user asks proofread file, act expert proofreader editor deep understanding clear, engaging, well-structured writing. Work paragraph paragraph, always starting making TODO list includes individual items top-level section. Fix spelling, grammar, minor problems without asking user. Label unclear, confusing, ambiguous sentences FIXME comment. report changed.

what: Chemistry: distinguish a molecule from its forms
title: Examples
text: CHEMROF LinkML model chemical entities, mixtures, related concepts. One examples models thalidomide two enantiomers racemic mixture. distinction matters. name “thalidomide” enough tell us whether record means chirality-agnostic molecule, one stereoisomer, mixture. example schema uses inheritance typed references: complete chemistry schema also declares molecular formulae, aliases, semantic mappings, enum chirality. Load compiled manifest inspect concrete classes: data use CURIEs identifiers. References contain identifier target record. Write three classes one batch. graft checks references existing store records staged batch. mixture can retrieved without flattening away distinction molecule stereoisomers: pattern also works salts, isotopes, mixtures, reaction participants, case chemistry-specific identity matters.
code: classes:   ChemicalEntity:     abstract: true     attributes:       id:         identifier: true         required: true         range: uriorcurie       name:         required: true    Enantiomer:     is_a: Molecule     attributes:       chirality:         range: ChiralityEnum         required: true       chirality_agnostic_form:         range: Molecule         required: true         inlined: false    RacemicMixture:     is_a: ChemicalEntity     attributes:       has_left_enantiomer:         range: Enantiomer         required: true         inlined: false       has_right_enantiomer:         range: Enantiomer         required: true         inlined: false library(graft)  chemistry_schema <- kg_schema(system.file(   "extdata",   "chemistry.graft.json",   package = "graft" ))  kg_classes(chemistry_schema) #>            class role statement_shape           table id_policy #> 1     Enantiomer node            <NA>      enantiomer   require #> 2       Molecule node            <NA>        molecule   require #> 3 RacemicMixture node            <NA> racemic_mixture   require kg_enums(chemistry_schema) #>            enum value meaning                description #> 1 ChiralityEnum  left    <NA>  Left-handed stereoisomer. #> 2 ChiralityEnum right    <NA> Right-handed stereoisomer. chemistry_records <- list(   Molecule = data.frame(     id = "chemistry:thalidomide",     name = "thalidomide",     molecular_formula = "C13H10N2O4"   ),   Enantiomer = data.frame(     id = c("chemistry:S-thalidomide", "chemistry:R-thalidomide"),     name = c("S-thalidomide", "R-thalidomide"),     molecular_formula = c("C13H10N2O4", "C13H10N2O4"),     chirality = c("left", "right"),     chirality_agnostic_form = c(       "chemistry:thalidomide",       "chemistry:thalidomide"     )   ),   RacemicMixture = data.frame(     id = "CHEBI:9513",     name = "racemic thalidomide",     aliases = I(list(c("thalidomide", "thalidomide racemate"))),     has_left_enantiomer = "chemistry:S-thalidomide",     has_right_enantiomer = "chemistry:R-thalidomide",     chirality_agnostic_form = "chemistry:thalidomide"   ) ) chemistry_store <- suppressMessages(   kg_connect_duckdb(chemistry_schema, ":memory:") ) kg_init(chemistry_store)  kg_ingest(   chemistry_store,   kg_batch(     producer = "chemistry-example",     idempotency_key = "thalidomide-v1"   ),   chemistry_records ) #> <kg_ingest_result> committed graft:01KXX4NSFYKQNY051JD0REH470 #>   inserted: 4 #>   updated:  0 #>   matched:  0 #>   observed: 4 mixture <- kg_get(chemistry_store, "CHEBI:9513")  mixture$record[c("id", "name")] #> $id #> [1] "CHEBI:9513" #>  #> $name #> [1] "racemic thalidomide" mixture$record[c(   "has_left_enantiomer",   "has_right_enantiomer",   "chirality_agnostic_form" )] #> $has_left_enantiomer #> [1] "chemistry:S-thalidomide" #>  #> $has_right_enantiomer #> [1] "chemistry:R-thalidomide" #>  #> $chirality_agnostic_form #> [1] "chemistry:thalidomide"

what: Biology: connect samples to the work performed on them
title: Examples
text: National Microbiome Data Collaborative schema uses LinkML describe environmental omics data. model separates studies, biosamples, data generation, workflow executions, data objects. smaller schema keeps three ideas: complete biology schema adds semantic mappings, coordinates, instrument details, controlled vocabulary analyte. records make provenance chain explicit: sequencing process belongs study takes particular biosample input. tabular hydrated interfaces answer different questions: pattern can connect specimens assays, genes phenotypes, organisms observations. LinkML supplies domain vocabulary constraints; graft supplies local storage retrieval layer.
code: classes:   Study:     is_a: NamedThing    Biosample:     is_a: NamedThing     attributes:       associated_studies:         range: Study         required: true         multivalued: true         inlined: false       ecosystem:       collection_site:    NucleotideSequencing:     is_a: DataGeneration     attributes:       has_input:         range: Biosample         required: true         multivalued: true         inlined: false       analyte_category:         range: AnalyteCategoryEnum         required: true biology_schema <- kg_schema(system.file(   "extdata",   "biology.graft.json",   package = "graft" ))  kg_classes(biology_schema) #>                  class role statement_shape                 table id_policy #> 1            Biosample node            <NA>             biosample   require #> 2 NucleotideSequencing node            <NA> nucleotide_sequencing   require #> 3                Study node            <NA>                 study   require kg_slots(biology_schema, "Biosample") #>        class               slot      range relational_type required multivalued #> 1  Biosample  alternative_names     string         VARCHAR    FALSE        TRUE #> 2  Biosample associated_studies      Study         VARCHAR     TRUE        TRUE #> 3  Biosample    collection_site     string         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 4  Biosample         created_at   datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #> 5  Biosample        description     string         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 6  Biosample          ecosystem     string         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 7  Biosample                 id uriorcurie         VARCHAR     TRUE       FALSE #> 8  Biosample           latitude      float          DOUBLE    FALSE       FALSE #> 9  Biosample          longitude      float          DOUBLE    FALSE       FALSE #> 10 Biosample               name     string         VARCHAR     TRUE       FALSE #> 11 Biosample         updated_at   datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #>    identifier object_reference enum          column #> 1       FALSE            FALSE <NA>            <NA> #> 2       FALSE             TRUE <NA>            <NA> #> 3       FALSE            FALSE <NA> collection_site #> 4       FALSE            FALSE <NA>      created_at #> 5       FALSE            FALSE <NA>     description #> 6       FALSE            FALSE <NA>       ecosystem #> 7        TRUE            FALSE <NA>              id #> 8       FALSE            FALSE <NA>        latitude #> 9       FALSE            FALSE <NA>       longitude #> 10      FALSE            FALSE <NA>            name #> 11      FALSE            FALSE <NA>      updated_at biology_records <- list(   Study = data.frame(     id = "nmdc:sty-11",     name = "Forest soil carbon study",     objective = "Relate soil conditions to microbial carbon cycling.",     ecosystem = "Environmental"   ),   Biosample = data.frame(     id = "nmdc:bsm-11",     name = "Forest soil core 11",     associated_studies = I(list("nmdc:sty-11")),     ecosystem = "Environmental",     collection_site = "Example forest plot",     latitude = 44.57,     longitude = -85.61   ),   NucleotideSequencing = data.frame(     id = "nmdc:dg-11",     name = "Soil metagenome sequencing",     associated_studies = I(list("nmdc:sty-11")),     has_input = I(list("nmdc:bsm-11")),     analyte_category = "metagenome",     instrument_name = "Illumina NovaSeq",     processing_institution = "Example sequencing center"   ) ) biology_store <- suppressMessages(   kg_connect_duckdb(biology_schema, ":memory:") ) kg_init(biology_store)  kg_ingest(   biology_store,   kg_batch(     producer = "biology-example",     idempotency_key = "soil-v1"   ),   biology_records ) #> <kg_ingest_result> committed graft:01KXX4NT9NRA0FPVEAZKT0VRRW #>   inserted: 3 #>   updated:  0 #>   matched:  0 #>   observed: 3 kg_records(biology_store, "Biosample") |>   dplyr::select(id, name, ecosystem, collection_site) |>   dplyr::collect() #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   id          name                ecosystem     collection_site     #>   <chr>       <chr>               <chr>         <chr>               #> 1 nmdc:bsm-11 Forest soil core 11 Environmental Example forest plot  sequencing <- kg_get(biology_store, "nmdc:dg-11") sequencing$record[c(   "name",   "analyte_category",   "has_input",   "associated_studies" )] #> $name #> [1] "Soil metagenome sequencing" #>  #> $analyte_category #> [1] "metagenome" #>  #> $has_input #> [1] "nmdc:bsm-11" #>  #> $associated_studies #> [1] "nmdc:sty-11"

what: Use the upstream model when it fits
title: Examples
text: examples adaptations, replacements CHEMROF NMDC. production project first ask whether established model already describes data. Compile upstream schema directly uses LinkML constructs graft supports classes match records need. Use smaller schema need well-defined subset, retain upstream class_uri, slot_uri, identifier mappings relationship remains visible. official LinkML examples page also points Biolink, NMDC, production models. LinkML Schema Registry useful looking existing model another domain.

title: Getting started with graft
text: main workflow : LinkML schema -> compiled .graft.json manifest -> DuckDB store -> R queries part one job: LinkML schema describes domain. define concrete classes materials, sources, claims, evidence extending graft’s core record roles. compiled manifest runtime contract. contains resolved classes, fields, relational mapping, identity rules, validation rules, graph projections, schema fingerprints. Commit project. DuckDB stores records. database remains table-native, portable, available familiar DBI dbplyr workflows. graft applies contract. Read write functions accept manifest-declared classes fields. Functions collect results require explicit limits report active schema digest. Python linkml-runtime used step 2. Loading existing manifest performing normal storage retrieval run entirely R.

title: Getting started with graft
text: graft currently available GitHub:
code: pak::pak("JamesHWade/graft")

title: Getting started with graft
text: package includes small materials contract guide. Loading compiled manifest initialize Python: Inspect contract creating store: role statement_shape columns important. let graft treat domain-specific classes consistently without pretending every record kind thing: nodes subjects objects workflow reasons ; narrative statements preserve source-faithful language; semantic statements express intentional subject-predicate-object assertion; sources describe information came ; evidence connects statement stored source locator.
code: library(graft) manifest <- system.file(   "extdata",   "materials.graft.json",   package = "graft" ) schema <- kg_schema(manifest) schema #> <kg_schema> materials 0.1.0 #>   classes:    5 #>   relations:  1 #>   structural: sha256:2f89cfc254e22a342a36ae87e438164fa714de11c712d57e5d7be25bfa00e145 kg_classes(schema) #>         class      role statement_shape       table     id_policy #> 1       Claim statement       narrative       claim          mint #> 2    Evidence  evidence            <NA>    evidence          mint #> 3    Material      node            <NA>    material resolve_exact #> 4 Measurement statement        semantic measurement          mint #> 5      Source    source            <NA>      source resolve_exact kg_slots(schema, "Claim") #>    class            slot             range relational_type required multivalued #> 1  Claim           about         GraftNode         VARCHAR     TRUE        TRUE #> 2  Claim     asserted_at          datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #> 3  Claim      claim_type         ClaimType         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 4  Claim      confidence             float          DOUBLE    FALSE       FALSE #> 5  Claim      created_at          datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #> 6  Claim              id        uriorcurie         VARCHAR     TRUE       FALSE #> 7  Claim        polarity StatementPolarity         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 8  Claim primary_subject         GraftNode         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 9  Claim  statement_text            string         VARCHAR     TRUE       FALSE #> 10 Claim          status   StatementStatus         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 11 Claim   superseded_by    GraftStatement         VARCHAR    FALSE       FALSE #> 12 Claim      updated_at          datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #> 13 Claim      valid_from          datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #> 14 Claim        valid_to          datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #>    identifier object_reference              enum          column #> 1       FALSE             TRUE              <NA>            <NA> #> 2       FALSE            FALSE              <NA>     asserted_at #> 3       FALSE            FALSE         ClaimType      claim_type #> 4       FALSE            FALSE              <NA>      confidence #> 5       FALSE            FALSE              <NA>      created_at #> 6        TRUE            FALSE              <NA>              id #> 7       FALSE            FALSE StatementPolarity        polarity #> 8       FALSE             TRUE              <NA> primary_subject #> 9       FALSE            FALSE              <NA>  statement_text #> 10      FALSE            FALSE   StatementStatus          status #> 11      FALSE             TRUE              <NA>   superseded_by #> 12      FALSE            FALSE              <NA>      updated_at #> 13      FALSE            FALSE              <NA>      valid_from #> 14      FALSE            FALSE              <NA>        valid_to

title: Getting started with graft
text: exploration, use -memory DuckDB database. durable store, replace ":memory:" file path. kg_init() creates tables graph views declared manifest, plus graft’s private metadata tables. Calling safe. existing store initialized structurally different manifest, graft reports schema mismatch rather silently changing meaning data.
code: store <- kg_connect_duckdb(schema, ":memory:") #> duckdb is keeping downloaded extensions in a temporary directory: #> ℹ /tmp/RtmpWPdhhy/duckdb/extensions #> This is removed when the R session ends, so extensions are re-downloaded each session. #> ℹ To keep them, point `options(duckdb.extension_directory =)` or the `DUCKDB_EXTENSION_DIRECTORY` environment variable at a permanent path. kg_init(store) store #> <kg_store> DuckDB initialized (read-write) #>   path:       :memory: #>   structural: sha256:2f89cfc254e22a342a36ae87e438164fa714de11c712d57e5d7be25bfa00e145

title: Getting started with graft
text: Ingestion happens atomic batches. batch identifies producer provides optional idempotency key, rerunning stage duplicate observations. First ingest one material one source: graft minted internal IDs registered schema-declared external identifiers. Exact lookup resolves normalized external values back stable records: Now add claim material. multivalued LinkML slot, , represented list-column: Finally, connect claim exact source passage: writes separate example lets graft mint ID later record refers . Producers already stable graft IDs can submit related classes one atomic kg_ingest() call.
code: foundations <- list(   Material = data.frame(     preferred_name = "Linear low-density polyethylene (LLDPE)",     description = "A polyethylene with controlled short-chain branching.",     cas_number = "9002-88-4"   ),   Source = data.frame(     uri = "https://example.org/lldpe-study",     title = "Controlled DSC study of LLDPE crystallinity",     doi = "10.1000/lldpe.dsc"   ) )  kg_ingest(   store,   kg_batch(     producer = "getting-started",     source_run_id = "materials-demo",     idempotency_key = "foundations-v1"   ),   foundations ) #> <kg_ingest_result> committed graft:01KXX4NXYNKQNY051JD0REH470 #>   inserted: 2 #>   updated:  0 #>   matched:  0 #>   observed: 2 material_id <- kg_lookup(   store,   namespace = "cas",   value = "CAS: 9002-88-4" )$record_id[[1]]  source_id <- kg_lookup(   store,   namespace = "doi",   value = "https://doi.org/10.1000/LLDPE.DSC" )$record_id[[1]] kg_write(   store,   kg_batch(     producer = "getting-started",     source_run_id = "materials-demo",     idempotency_key = "claim-v1"   ),   class = "Claim",   records = data.frame(     statement_text = paste(       "A controlled DSC experiment measured 37% crystallinity",       "for the LLDPE sample."     ),     primary_subject = material_id,     claim_type = "finding",     polarity = "positive",     confidence = 0.95,     status = "active",     about = I(list(material_id))   ) ) #> <kg_ingest_result> committed graft:01KXX4NYAEQ9QP8ZR98JRJEWGE #>   inserted: 1 #>   updated:  0 #>   matched:  0 #>   observed: 1  claim_id <- kg_find(   store,   "crystallinity",   class = "Claim",   limit = 1 )$id[[1]] kg_write(   store,   kg_batch(     producer = "getting-started",     source_run_id = "materials-demo",     idempotency_key = "evidence-v1"   ),   class = "Evidence",   records = data.frame(     statement_id = claim_id,     source_id = source_id,     support_type = "supports",     locator_type = "other",     locator_value = "table 1",     page_start = 5L,     page_end = 5L,     excerpt = "Crystallinity (%) for the LLDPE sample: 37."   ) ) #> <kg_ingest_result> committed graft:01KXX4NYJ7SE4FJ30WRC1PCJYS #>   inserted: 1 #>   updated:  0 #>   matched:  0 #>   observed: 1

what: Retrieve an answer and its evidence
title: Getting started with graft
text: Search useful discovery: Hydration starts one stable ID returns bounded related records: Claims remain assertions rather collapsed single truth. status, polarity, qualifiers, evidence stay available inspection: distinction deliberate. narrative claim records source says. semantic statement records intentional normalized assertion. Evidence can support contradict either statement shape, graft can return competing claims without adjudicating user’s behalf.
code: kg_find(store, "LLDPE", limit = 5) #>                                 id    class #> 1 graft:01KXX4NXZNBN89156BSVND46JB   Source #> 2 graft:01KXX4NXZA539065NJR1Z70V06 Material #> 3 graft:01KXX4NYB1V7B2F7BQ16HSZV0M    Claim #>                                                                          label #> 1                                  Controlled DSC study of LLDPE crystallinity #> 2                                      Linear low-density polyethylene (LLDPE) #> 3 A controlled DSC experiment measured 37% crystallinity for the LLDPE sample. #>   score #> 1     6 #> 2     5 #> 3     3 material <- kg_get(store, material_id) material #> <kg_record> Material graft:01KXX4NXZA539065NJR1Z70V06 #>   identifiers: 1 #>   claims: 1 #>   evidence: 1  material$record[c("preferred_name", "cas_number")] #> $preferred_name #> [1] "Linear low-density polyethylene (LLDPE)" #>  #> $cas_number #> [1] "9002-88-4" claims <- kg_claims(store, material_id) claims[c("statement_text", "confidence", "status")] #>                                                                 statement_text #> 1 A controlled DSC experiment measured 37% crystallinity for the LLDPE sample. #>   confidence status #> 1       0.95 active  evidence <- kg_evidence(store, claim_id) evidence[c(   "support_type",   "source_title",   "locator_value",   "excerpt" )] #>   support_type                                source_title locator_value #> 1     supports Controlled DSC study of LLDPE crystallinity       table 1 #>                                       excerpt #> 1 Crystallinity (%) for the LLDPE sample: 37.

title: Getting started with graft
text: graft offers different interfaces different jobs: example, kg_records() returns lazy dbplyr table: kg_select() safer collected interface caller choose fields filters must provide SQL:
code: kg_records(store, "Claim") |>   dplyr::select(id, statement_text, confidence) |>   dplyr::collect() #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   id                               statement_text                     confidence #>   <chr>                            <chr>                                   <dbl> #> 1 graft:01KXX4NYB1V7B2F7BQ16HSZV0M A controlled DSC experiment measu…       0.95 kg_select(   store,   class = "Claim",   fields = c("id", "statement_text", "confidence"),   filters = list(list(     field = "confidence",     operator = "gte",     value = 0.9   )),   limit = 10 ) #>                                 id #> 1 graft:01KXX4NYB1V7B2F7BQ16HSZV0M #>                                                                 statement_text #> 1 A controlled DSC experiment measured 37% crystallinity for the LLDPE sample. #>   confidence #> 1       0.95

title: Getting started with graft
text: ellmer package installed, kg_tools() creates six read-tools capture one initialized store: tools call query functions shown . accept SQL, file paths, URLs, network options. result reports limit, whether truncated, store’s schema digest. kg_tools() validate model output. gives model read-access records evidence already present store.
code: chat <- ellmer::chat_anthropic() chat$set_tools(kg_tools(store))

title: Getting started with graft
text: LinkML schema article starts ordinary PersonInfo-style schema. compiles without graft annotations import graft’s core schema. installed materials example shows optional enriched form: Extend graft’s core roles domain needs package-specific behavior claims, evidence, sources, mentions, graph edges: GraftNode GraftEdge GraftNarrativeStatement GraftSemanticStatement GraftSource GraftEvidence GraftMention GraftMetadata Annotations can declare identity policies, external identifier namespaces, search fields, label fields, origin keys, semantic qualifiers. Compile schema : Commit source schema compiled manifest. Application production environments can load manifest without Python:
code: system.file(   "extdata",   "materials.linkml.yaml",   package = "graft" ) #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/graft/extdata/materials.linkml.yaml" kg_compile_schema(   "my-domain.linkml.yaml",   "my-domain.graft.json" ) schema <- kg_schema("my-domain.graft.json") store <- kg_connect_duckdb(schema, "knowledge.duckdb") kg_init(store)

title: Getting started with graft
text: Use graft project needs preserve record identity, claims, sources, evidence across runs keeping data available R, DBI, dbplyr. graft design LinkML schema, collect source material, provide vector similarity search, replace general-purpose graph database. manages boundary schema-defined records representation DuckDB.

title: Use a LinkML schema with graft
text: complete schema : standard LinkML features: classes define Person Organization records; attributes define fields; identifier: true marks class identifier; required, range, minimum_value add constraints; multivalued: true represents repeated values; slot_uri class_uri connect local names Schema.org terms. graft-specific authoring requirement file.
code: library(graft)  schema_file <- system.file(   "extdata",   "personinfo.linkml.yaml",   package = "graft" ) id: https://w3id.org/graft/examples/personinfo name: personinfo title: Person information description: A small people and organizations schema based on LinkML PersonInfo. version: 0.1.0 prefixes:   linkml: https://w3id.org/linkml/   personinfo: https://w3id.org/graft/examples/personinfo/   sdo: https://schema.org/ imports:   - linkml:types default_prefix: personinfo default_range: string  classes:   Person:     class_uri: sdo:Person     attributes:       id:         identifier: true         required: true         slot_uri: sdo:identifier       full_name:         required: true         slot_uri: sdo:name       aliases:         multivalued: true         slot_uri: sdo:alternateName       age:         range: integer         minimum_value: 0       employed_by:         range: Organization         multivalued: true         inlined: false         slot_uri: sdo:worksFor    Organization:     class_uri: sdo:Organization     attributes:       id:         identifier: true         required: true         slot_uri: sdo:identifier       name:         required: true         slot_uri: sdo:name

title: Use a LinkML schema with graft
text: Compile LinkML file graft manifest: Compilation step uses Python linkml-runtime. Commit generated .graft.json file source schema. Applications can load manifest without Python. package includes compiled copy rest article executable: plain LinkML class, graft supplies conservative storage defaults: concrete class becomes node table; scalar string-like id remains record identifier; scalar attributes become columns; multivalued attributes become related tables; class-valued attributes become references; common text fields provide display label basic search fields; created_at updated_at added runtime manifest. defaults affect compiled manifest, source LinkML file.
code: kg_compile_schema(   schema_file,   "personinfo.graft.json" ) manifest_file <- system.file(   "extdata",   "personinfo.graft.json",   package = "graft" ) schema <- kg_schema(manifest_file)  kg_classes(schema) #>          class role statement_shape        table id_policy #> 1 Organization node            <NA> organization   require #> 2       Person node            <NA>       person   require kg_slots(schema, "Person") #>    class        slot        range relational_type required multivalued #> 1 Person         age      integer          BIGINT    FALSE       FALSE #> 2 Person     aliases       string         VARCHAR    FALSE        TRUE #> 3 Person  created_at     datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #> 4 Person employed_by Organization         VARCHAR    FALSE        TRUE #> 5 Person   full_name       string         VARCHAR     TRUE       FALSE #> 6 Person          id       string         VARCHAR     TRUE       FALSE #> 7 Person  updated_at     datetime       TIMESTAMP    FALSE       FALSE #>   identifier object_reference enum     column #> 1      FALSE            FALSE <NA>        age #> 2      FALSE            FALSE <NA>       <NA> #> 3      FALSE            FALSE <NA> created_at #> 4      FALSE             TRUE <NA>       <NA> #> 5      FALSE            FALSE <NA>  full_name #> 6       TRUE            FALSE <NA>         id #> 7      FALSE            FALSE <NA> updated_at

title: Use a LinkML schema with graft
text: Use file path instead ":memory:" store persist across R sessions.
code: store <- suppressMessages(kg_connect_duckdb(schema, ":memory:")) kg_init(store)

title: Use a LinkML schema with graft
text: input data use class attribute names LinkML schema. object reference contains identifier referenced record. multivalued attribute uses list-column. two classes committed one transaction. reference Person.employed_by checked records already store records staged batch.
code: records <- list(   Organization = data.frame(     id = "org:daily-planet",     name = "Daily Planet"   ),   Person = data.frame(     id = "person:clark-kent",     full_name = "Clark Kent",     aliases = I(list(c("Superman", "Kal-El"))),     age = 35L,     employed_by = I(list("org:daily-planet"))   ) )  kg_ingest(   store,   kg_batch(     producer = "linkml-example",     idempotency_key = "personinfo-v1"   ),   records ) #> <kg_ingest_result> committed graft:01KXX4P2SAKQNY051JD0REH470 #>   inserted: 2 #>   updated:  0 #>   matched:  0 #>   observed: 2

title: Use a LinkML schema with graft
text: kg_records() returns lazy dbplyr table: inferred text fields available kg_find(): kg_get() hydrates record multivalued fields:
code: people <- kg_records(store, "Person")  people |>   dplyr::select(id, full_name, age) |>   dplyr::collect() #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   id                full_name    age #>   <chr>             <chr>      <dbl> #> 1 person:clark-kent Clark Kent    35 kg_find(store, "Clark", class = "Person", limit = 5) #>                  id  class      label score #> 1 person:clark-kent Person Clark Kent     6 person <- kg_get(store, "person:clark-kent")  person$record[c("id", "full_name", "age")] #> $id #> [1] "person:clark-kent" #>  #> $full_name #> [1] "Clark Kent" #>  #> $age #> [1] 35 person$record$aliases #> [1] "Kal-El"   "Superman" person$record$employed_by #> [1] "org:daily-planet"

what: Add graft semantics only when needed
title: Use a LinkML schema with graft
text: Ordinary LinkML describes record structure, identifiers, ranges, cardinality, semantic mappings. enough baseline storage querying. knowledge-store decisions part general LinkML schema. example, project may want distinguish narrative claims normalized semantic statements, connect claims exact evidence locations, normalize external identifiers, choose weighted search fields. cases, import graft-core.linkml extend one graft’s optional roles: GraftNarrativeStatement GraftSemanticStatement GraftSource GraftEvidence GraftMention GraftEdge GraftMetadata package’s materials.linkml.yaml shows enriched form. Start ordinary LinkML add roles application needs behavior.

Graft therefore refuses construct adapter Tempest exposes complete, versioned public serialization contract. use internal Tempest helpers, fabricate specification, store opaque R serialization.

title: graft 0.0.0.9000
text: kg_batch() creates stable producer batches, kg_ingest() atomically reconciles, validates, upserts multiple record classes identifier, origin, observation, replay lineage. kg_claims(), kg_evidence(), kg_competing_claims() retrieve bounded narrative semantic assertions, stored citations, non-adjudicated comparison sets preserving qualifiers ordinary attributes. kg_compile_schema() compiles ordinary LinkML schemas deterministic, portable graft manifests without requiring graft-specific imports annotations; graft core roles remain available richer claim, evidence, graph behavior. kg_connect_duckdb(), kg_init(), kg_disconnect() provide ownership-aware DuckDB store lifecycle manifest-driven initialization structural schema protection. kg_context() generates token-bounded, sensitive-field-safe description active manifest DuckDB access constraints. kg_edges(), kg_nodes(), kg_neighbors(), kg_traverse(), kg_subgraph() provide lazy manifest-driven graph projections plus deterministic, explicitly collected one-hop two-hop retrieval hard node edge caps. kg_find(), kg_lookup(), kg_identifiers() provide bounded manifest-declared search exact identifier resolution registry provenance. kg_get() hydrates exactly one public record bounded related identifiers, claims, evidence. kg_ingest_tempest_records() commits mapped Tempest domain records run- stage-stable idempotency keys, independently Tempest deliverable persistence. kg_records() returns lazy typed dbplyr tables public concrete classes. kg_schema(), kg_classes(), kg_slots(), kg_enums(), kg_schema_info() load inspect manifests without Python. kg_schema_diff() reports structural schema changes. kg_select() provides collected, hard-capped structured query surface manifest validation arbitrary SQL. kg_store_info() kg_capabilities() inspect DuckDB stores without requiring Python. kg_tools() creates six read-ellmer tools bounded Graft retrieval APIs, structured results arbitrary SQL surface. kg_unresolved() returns bounded unresolved mention records. kg_validate_data() preflights staged identity, shape, reference checks ingestion without mutating store. kg_write() provides one-class convenience wrapper kg_ingest(). tempest_artifact_store_graft() explicitly reports upstream Tempest serialization contract required durable typed-artifact persistence can supported.
